name: HashiCorp Cloud Platform (HCP) Vault API
what_it_does: HashiCorp Cloud Platform managed Vault with REST API — providing fully managed secrets management (no self-hosted Vault to operate) with the same Vault API surface for storing and accessing secrets, dynamic credentials generation, encryption as a service, and PKI, with HCP-specific management API for cluster lifecycle and billing.
use_cases: Storing and retrieving secrets via HCP Vault API for application configuration management
Generating dynamic cloud credentials via HCP Vault API for AWS/GCP/Azure ephemeral access
Managing PKI certificates via HCP Vault API for internal TLS and certificate automation
Encrypting sensitive data via HCP Vault Transit API for application-layer encryption
Managing HCP Vault clusters via HCP management API for infrastructure-as-code provisioning
not_for: Self-hosted Vault with full control (use open-source Vault or Vault Enterprise for self-hosted)
Simple API key storage only (use AWS Secrets Manager or 1Password Secrets Automation for simpler needs)
Non-HashiCorp stack (consider AWS Secrets Manager if fully AWS-native)
best_when: A team wants Vault's powerful secrets capabilities (dynamic credentials, PKI, transit encryption) without the operational burden of running and upgrading self-hosted Vault clusters.
avoid_when: You need full Vault feature set (custom plugins), on-premises deployment, or want to avoid HCP vendor lock-in.

auth.notes: Two auth layers: (1) HCP management API: HCP service principal credentials → OAuth2 access token for cluster management. (2) Vault API: Vault-native token (client_token from Vault auth methods: AppRole, AWS, Kubernetes, OIDC, etc.). HCP Vault: API identical to self-hosted Vault — same endpoints. Vault token: X-Vault-Token header. HCP service principal: HMAC-based credentials for HCP management. Vault admin token: obtained via HCP admin token generation. Vault SDKs: official Go, Python, Ruby, Java, C#, JS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Development tier: single cluster, limited operations
pricing.notes: HashiCorp (acquired by IBM 2024 for $6.4B). HCP: HashiCorp Cloud Platform — managed services for Vault, Consul, Terraform, Packer. HCP Vault: same Vault API surface as self-hosted. Two cluster types: HCP Vault Dedicated (fully managed, private), HCP Vault Secrets (new simpler secrets service). Vault Secrets (HCP-native): simpler API, different from full Vault. Pricing per hour for cluster uptime. Multi-region replication available. TerraForm integration: manage HCP Vault via Terraform. IBM acquisition: product direction may evolve under IBM ownership.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on cluster tier; Standard: ~50K ops/sec

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Two auth layers: HCP service principal + Vault token (separate). HCP Vault Secrets ≠ HCP Vault Dedicated (different APIs). IBM acquisition 2024. X-Vault-Token header. Official SDKs in 6 languages. FedRAMP authorized. $0.03-0.42/hr pricing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Vault reads idempotent. Dynamic credential generation: each request generates new unique credentials. KV writes: idempotent (overwrite). Transit encryption: deterministic encryption available. PKI: idempotent by certificate serial.
known_agent_gotchas: TWO auth layers: (1) HCP service principal for cluster management; (2) Vault token for secret operations — different credentials
HCP Vault Secrets (new product) vs HCP Vault Dedicated: different APIs, endpoints, and capabilities
Vault token auth methods: choose AppRole for service accounts, Kubernetes auth for K8s pods — not static tokens
X-Vault-Token: never log this header — Vault root token is an extremely sensitive credential
Dynamic credentials (AWS/GCP/Azure): each request returns NEW ephemeral credentials — manage credential lifecycle
IBM acquisition (2024): verify licensing and BSL (Business Source License) implications for your use case

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Vault token system: fine-grained policies, TTLs, renewable tokens. SOC2/ISO27001/FedRAMP authorized. Dynamic credentials: ephemeral credentials minimize breach window. Seal/unseal: auto-unseal via cloud KMS. Vault audit log: complete operation audit trail. HCP: HashiCorp manages HSMs for seal keys. IBM acquisition: security posture historically strong — verify post-acquisition. BSL license: open-source no longer (switched from MPL-2.0 in 2023). Official SDKs handle token lifecycle securely.
